title: FAQ: International Remote Work Guidelines
body:
Q: How long can an employee work remotely from outside their home country?
A: Under the current policy, the maximum allowed remote work period from an international location is 60 days within a 12-month rolling period. Employees must notify their manager and local HR before departure. For any extension beyond 60 days, a special exception must be filed with the business unit head, but such extensions are rarely granted. Please note that this policy is under review and may be updated.

title: International Remote Work Policy (v3.1)
body:
1. Purpose
This policy defines the terms under which employees may work remotely from a country other than their primary work location.

2. Maximum Duration
Employees are permitted to work remotely from an international location for up to 90 days in total per calendar year. This period does not need to be consecutive.

3. Approval Requirements
All international remote work arrangements must have written approval from both the employee's direct manager and HR Business Partner before the travel begins. The approval must specify the exact dates and location, and must be submitted at least two weeks in advance.

4. Tax and Legal Compliance
Employees must ensure their planned remote work complies with host-country tax, immigration, and employment laws. HR will coordinate a review with the Global Mobility team.

5. Equipment and Security
Standard IT security policies apply; VPN and approved devices are mandatory.

6. Exceptions
Any request to exceed the 90-day limit requires VP-level approval and will be assessed on a case-by-case basis.

This policy supersedes all previous remote work duration limits and takes effect immediately.

---

title: Travel and Expense Reimbursement Procedure
body:
This article outlines the standard procedure for submitting travel and expense reports.

1. All expenses must be submitted within 30 days of incurring the cost.
2. Receipts are required for any single expense over $25.
3. Air travel must be booked through the corporate travel portal unless an exception is approved by the manager.
4. Per diem rates are set per country and are published in the Finance portal.
5. Any non-compliant submissions will be returned for correction and may delay reimbursement.

title: Office Equipment Checkout Policy
body:
Employees who need temporary equipment (laptops, monitors, etc.) for business travel or remote work may check out items from the IT inventory.

- Requests must be submitted via the IT Service Portal at least 3 business days in advance.
- Standard checkout period is 30 days, renewable with justification.
- All equipment must be returned within 5 days of the checkout period ending.
- Damaged or lost equipment will be subject to a replacement fee as outlined in the Asset Management Policy.
